chapters.title: Introduction and Recurrent Themes
chapters.notes: Paper's purpose, methodology (recorded lessons), and overview of Bernstein's informal, context-driven teaching approach.

chapters.title: Jazz as a Language and the Importance of Narrative
chapters.notes: Opens the Concepts section; jazz improvisation as conversation, vocabulary, and storytelling vs. scales-over-chords pedagogy.

chapters.title: How to Learn a Tune
chapters.notes: Bernstein's oral-tradition approach to learning tunes; analyzing structural notes, harmony, and melody together.

chapters.title: The Primacy of the Melody and Its Relation to Chords
chapters.notes: Covers 'The primacy of the melody' (p6), 'The relation between chords and melody' (p7), and 'Language, rhythm and meaning' (p8) — grouped as a melody-centered unit.

chapters.title: Comping and Supporting the Melody
chapters.notes: Comping fundamentals: 'Comping and supporting the melody' (p9), 'Dressing the notes' (p9), 'Spend time comping' (p11), and 'Rhythm of the comping' (p11).

chapters.title: Harmonic Movement, Tension and Substitutions
chapters.notes: Tension/resolution as the heart of jazz harmony; passing chords, tritone substitutions, and voice leading.

chapters.title: Expanding the Vocabulary
chapters.notes: Major umbrella section with three sub-methods: 'An open approach' (p14), 'Creating ideas around target notes' (p15), and 'Creating ideas from a shape' (p21).

chapters.title: Melodic Target Notes and the Narrative of the Solo
chapters.notes: Covers 'Melodic target notes' (p24) and 'The narrative of the solo' (p25) — applying vocabulary-building to solo construction.

chapters.title: Cadenza, Technique and Flexibility
chapters.notes: Covers 'Cadenza' (p26) and 'Technique and flexibility: Dynamics, Phrasing and Articulation' (p27).

chapters.title: Final Thoughts
chapters.notes: Concluding reflections: 'Unique and narrow aesthetic path developed over the years' (p29) and 'A pure exponent of the jazz tradition' (p30).
